description: Drag-and-drop block programming for Arduino — build programs visually and Blocks Editor writes the C++ for you. Works with PlatformIO and the Arduino CLI.
config.title: Blocks Editor
config.generateOnChange: Generate code automatically whenever blocks change. When disabled, code is generated only when the Generate Code button is pressed.
config.showMinimap: Show a minimap overview of the block workspace in the bottom-right corner.
config.catalogPaths: Local directories or remote URLs for block catalog YAML files. Local paths (relative to the workspace root, or absolute) are scanned recursively. URLs (http/https) are downloaded to the project .blocks/ folder on first load; use the "Refresh Remote Catalogs" command to force re-download.
command.enableClaudeCode: Blocks Editor: Enable Claude Code integration
chat.blockAuthor.description: Create block catalog YAML files for hardware libraries
chat.command.research: Research a hardware library for block catalog creation
chat.command.design: Design blocks based on gathered research
chat.command.generate: Generate YAML catalog from the design
chat.command.validate: Validate YAML catalog against the schema
tool.fetchUrl.description: Fetch any URL and return its text content. Use for reading library documentation, GitHub raw files (.h, .cpp, library.properties), and example sketches.
tool.searchPio.description: Search the PlatformIO library registry by name. Returns matching libraries with versions and descriptions.
tool.checkArduino.description: Check if a library is in the Arduino Library Registry (installable via arduino-cli lib install). PIO and Arduino registries do not fully overlap — check both.
tool.validateCatalog.description: Validate multi-document YAML against the block catalog schema and run structural checks (duplicate types, precedence, placeholders). Always validate before saving.
tool.saveCatalog.userDescription: Save a block catalog YAML file to the workspace .blocks/ directory
tool.saveCatalog.description: Save a YAML catalog file to the workspace .blocks/ directory. The extension auto-reloads catalogs when new files appear.
viewContainer.catalogBrowser.title: Block Catalogs
view.catalogRegistry.name: Community Catalog
config.catalogRegistryUrl: URL of the community block catalog index (index.json). Change to use a private or self-hosted registry.
walkthrough.title: Get Started with Blocks Editor
walkthrough.description: Learn how to build Arduino programs visually with drag-and-drop blocks.
walkthrough.openWithBlocks.title: Open a file with Blocks Editor
walkthrough.openWithBlocks.description:
Right-click any `.cpp` or `.ino` file → **Open With…** → **Blocks Editor**.

Drag blocks to build your program — the C++ code is generated automatically.
walkthrough.arduinoCli.title: Compile & upload with Arduino CLI IDE
walkthrough.arduinoCli.description:
Blocks Editor focuses on visual programming — it doesn't compile or flash your board. Install the companion **Arduino CLI IDE** extension to compile, upload, and monitor your sketch right from the editor toolbar, without leaving VS Code.

[Install Arduino CLI IDE](command:extension.open?%22linucs.vscode-arduino-cli-ide%22)
walkthrough.authorBlocks.title: Author custom blocks
walkthrough.authorBlocks.description:
Create block catalogs for new hardware libraries. Use the **@blocks** chat participant or write YAML by hand.

[Enable Claude Code integration](command:blocks-editor.enableClaudeCodeIntegration)
walkthrough.communityCatalog.title: Browse community catalogs
walkthrough.communityCatalog.description:
Discover and download block catalogs contributed by the community.

[Open Community Catalog](command:blocks-editor.catalogRegistry.focus)
walkthrough.contribute.title: Help others learn — contribute
walkthrough.contribute.description:
I believe Arduino and block programming can truly help beginners learn advanced concepts — including edge AI. But I'm a solo developer, and I can't author blocks for every hardware device and use case out there.

If you also believe in sharing knowledge and helping others learn, **please contribute**: submit your block catalogs, open issues, or send pull requests. Every contribution makes visual programming accessible to more people.

[Share your Blocks](https://github.com/linucs/blocks-community-catalog)
